---
title: "DUS_heatmap"
author: "Oliwia Hillestad Waz"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---


```{r Load packages, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(Biostrings) 
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2) 
library(tidyr) 
library(dplyr)  
library(forcats) # to arrange order 
library(openxlsx) # to export as excel table 

```


## Chromo-DUS search ##

Defining DUS-motifs:
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
dus10 <- "GTTGGCCGMA" #M depicts an allowed variation between A and C 
dus9 <- "GTTGGCCGM" 
dus8 <- "GTTGGCCG" 
dus7 <- "TTGGCCG"
dus6 <- "TGGCCG"
dus5 <- "GGCCG"
dus4 <- "GCCG"


```



```{r}
#Load fasta file into R 
dna_seqs <- readDNAStringSet("C:/Users/oliwi/OneDrive - OsloMet/00. Master Thesies/Bioinformatics/Data/Chromo36_ecoli_pseudomallei.fasta") 

# Define the two genomes with contigs to target them for merging later on 
merge_targets <- c("Vogesella indigofera", "Gulbenkiania indica", "Burkholderia pseudomallei") 

# Create a grouping ID; this ID is ONLY used to decide which contigs belong togethe
grouping_id <- sapply(names(dna_seqs), function(header) { 
  is_merge_target <- any(sapply(merge_targets, function(x) grepl(x, header))) 
  if (is_merge_target) { 
    header %>% str_remove("^\\S+\\s+") %>%  # Remove Accession (NZ_...) 
      str_remove(",.*$") %>%   # Remove everything after comma 
      str_remove("_\\d+$") %>% # Remove contig suffix (e.g., _101)
      str_remove("(?i)chromosome\\s*\\d+") %>% # Remove "chromosome 1" or "chromosome 2"
      str_trim() } else {   
        header } # keep other genomes as is 
  }) 

# Merge sequences 
split_seqs <- split(dna_seqs, grouping_id) 
seqs <- DNAStringSet( lapply(split_seqs, 
                             function(x) do.call(
                               xscat, as.list(x)))) 

# Final Cleaning of the names - remove the keys (which still have the Genus)
names(seqs) <- names(split_seqs) %>%
  str_remove("^[A-Z]{1,3}_[A-Z0-9]+\\.\\d+\\s+") %>%  
  str_remove("(?i)\\s+chromosome.*$") %>% 
  str_remove("(?i)\\s+complete genome.*$") %>% 
  str_remove("(?i)\\s+whole genome shotgun.*$") %>% 
  str_remove(",.*$") %>%   
  ifelse(grepl("^Vogesella", .), 
         str_remove(., "\\s+Ga\\d+$"), .) %>%   # Remove Ga0215675 ONLY from Vogesella
  ifelse(grepl("^Gulbenkiania", .), 
         str_remove(., "\\s+DSM_\\d+$"), .) %>% # Remove DSM_17901 ONLY from Gulbenkiania
  str_trim()

# Verification 
print(names(seqs))
```


Count motifs 
```{r}
# Function that counts DUS for both forward and reverse. fixed = FALSE allows R to interpret IUPAC codes like 'M' which stand for ether bp A or C
count_motif <- function(seqset, motif) {
  forward <- vcountPattern(motif, seqset, fixed = FALSE)
  rev_motif <- as.character(reverseComplement(DNAString(motif)))
  reverse <- vcountPattern(rev_motif, seqset, fixed = FALSE)
  return(forward + reverse)
} 

# counts per genome in a table split by name  
counts_df <- data.frame(Genome = names(seqs),   
  DUS10 = count_motif(seqs, dus10),
  DUS9 = count_motif(seqs, dus9),   
  DUS8 = count_motif(seqs, dus8),
  DUS7 = count_motif(seqs, dus7),
  DUS6 = count_motif(seqs, dus6),
  DUS5 = count_motif(seqs, dus5),
  DUS4 = count_motif(seqs, dus4)
  )   

counts_df <- counts_df %>% 
  filter(!grepl("plasmid", Genome, ignore.case = TRUE)) #removing plasmids

#export to excel to get a list of all the strains and not log10 adjusted counts
#write.xlsx(counts_df, file = "Strain_list.xlsx", rowNames = FALSE)

```


Establish DUS/Mb and not just raw counts 
```{r}
genome_lengths <- data.frame(
  Genome = names(seqs),
  Length_Mb = width(seqs) / 1e6
)

# Add genome lengths
counts_df <- merge(counts_df, genome_lengths, by = "Genome")

# Calculate normalised counts (DUS per Mb)
counts_df$DUS10_perMb <- counts_df$DUS10 / counts_df$Length_Mb
counts_df$DUS9_perMb <- counts_df$DUS9 / counts_df$Length_Mb
counts_df$DUS8_perMb <- counts_df$DUS8 / counts_df$Length_Mb
counts_df$DUS7_perMb <- counts_df$DUS7 / counts_df$Length_Mb
counts_df$DUS6_perMb <- counts_df$DUS6 / counts_df$Length_Mb
counts_df$DUS5_perMb <- counts_df$DUS5 / counts_df$Length_Mb
counts_df$DUS4_perMb <- counts_df$DUS4 / counts_df$Length_Mb

# reshape normalised data
chromo_long <- counts_df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = ends_with("_perMb"),
    names_to = "Motif",
    values_to = "Count_perMb"
  ) %>%
  mutate(Motif = gsub("_perMb", "", Motif)) %>%
  distinct(Genome, Motif, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(Genome = fct_reorder(Genome, Count_perMb, .desc = FALSE))
```


visualise as a heatmap
```{r}
# Set order of x-axis
chromo_long$Motif <- factor(
  chromo_long$Motif,
  levels = c("DUS10", "DUS9", "DUS8", "DUS7", "DUS6", "DUS5", "DUS4")
)


# Heat-map visualising DUS/Mb for merged dataset  
ggplot(chromo_long,
       aes(x = Motif, y = Genome, fill = Count_perMb)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(Count_perMb, 1)), color = "black", size = 3) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colours = c("ghostwhite","ghostwhite","lightsteelblue", "lightsteelblue3", "lightsteelblue4"),
    trans = "log10",
    breaks = c(1, 10, 100, 1000, 10000),
    labels = scales::comma
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "DUS/Mb in Chromobacteriaceae",
    subtitle = "Values include E. coli K-12 and B. pseudomallei as controlls",
    x = "Motif",
    y = "Strain",
    fill = "DUS/Mb (log10)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )


```
